Recommended_Architecture: Flat structure with main.rs and handlers/
Error_Strategy: Custom error type implementing IntoResponse; use thiserror for enum variants
Concurrency_Strategy: Single task per request; use tower middleware for cross-cutting
Testing_Strategy: Integration tests with axum::test; unit tests for service logic
Anti_Patterns: God handler functions, .unwrap() everywhere, blocking in async

Recommended_Architecture: Clean Architecture: src/handlers/ src/services/ src/repositories/ src/models/ src/errors/
Package_Priority: axum, sqlx, serde, tokio, tower, tower-http, tracing, utoipa
Error_Strategy: Custom error enum with thiserror; IntoResponse for HTTP mapping; anyhow in internal layers
Concurrency_Strategy: Worker pool with JoinSet for background tasks; tokio::spawn for parallel I/O
Testing_Strategy: Integration tests with testcontainers; unit tests with mockall traits
Anti_Patterns: Circular dependencies between modules, database logic in handlers, global mutable state, clone abuse

Recommended_Architecture: Layered: src/handlers/ src/services/ src/repositories/ src/models/ migrations/
Package_Priority: axum, sqlx (postgres + runtime-tokio), serde, tokio, tracing
Error_Strategy: Wrap sqlx::Error into domain errors; handle constraint violations; use .context() for chains
Concurrency_Strategy: Connection pool via PgPool; use context/timeout on all queries
Testing_Strategy: Test with testcontainers-rs/postgres; use sqlx test macros
Anti_Patterns: N+1 queries, missing indexes, not using compile-time checked queries, SQL injection via format!

Error_Strategy: Wrap mongodb::error::Error; handle not-found explicitly; use typed results
Concurrency_Strategy: Use connection pooling via mongodb::Client; reuse across handlers
Testing_Strategy: Test with testcontainers-rs/mongo; seed test data in fixtures
Anti_Patterns: Storing highly relational data in MongoDB, unbounded Vec growth in documents, missing indexes

Error_Strategy: Print errors to stderr with process::exit(1); use anyhow::Context for wrapping
Testing_Strategy: Test with assert_cmd and predicates; extract logic into lib.rs
Anti_Patterns: Printing errors to stdout, using .unwrap() in main, not setting exit codes

Recommended_Architecture: src/lib.rs src/cli.rs src/commands/ with clap subcommands
Package_Priority: clap, dialoguer, indicatif, console, anyhow, serde, tokio
Error_Strategy: Structured error types per subcommand; aggregate errors for batch operations
Concurrency_Strategy: Use tokio for async I/O; rayon for CPU-heavy parallel operations
Testing_Strategy: Test each subcommand with assert_cmd; test config loading; use insta for output snapshots
Anti_Patterns: Deeply nested subcommands, mixing UI and business logic, not handling SIGINT/SIGTERM

Package_Priority: tonic, prost, tonic-build, sqlx, tokio, tracing, tracing-opentelemetry
Error_Strategy: Return tonic::Status codes; map domain errors to codes; use interceptors for cross-cutting
Concurrency_Strategy: Per-RPC task managed by tonic; use streaming for large payloads
Testing_Strategy: Test with tonic::transport::Channel; mock services with mockall
Anti_Patterns: Huge protobuf messages, not using interceptors, blocking in stream handlers

Package_Priority: axum, askama or tera, sqlx, tower-http, tokio, tracing
Error_Strategy: Custom error pages; flash messages for user errors; log internal errors
Concurrency_Strategy: Standard task-per-request; use tower middleware for sessions
Testing_Strategy: Test handlers with axum::test; test template rendering; browser tests
Anti_Patterns: Template injection via unescaped input, not using tower-http for static files, missing CSRF

Package_Priority: No external deps preferred; use std only where possible
Error_Strategy: Return typed errors with thiserror; never panic; never log
Concurrency_Strategy: Document Send/Sync safety; use sync primitives; make concurrency opt-in
Testing_Strategy: 100% test coverage on pub API; doc tests; proptest for edge cases; fuzz targets
Anti_Patterns: Logging to stdout, panicking on errors, exposing internal types, unstable API before 1.0

Error_Strategy: Send error frames to client; close with appropriate codes; log server-side errors
Concurrency_Strategy: Hub pattern: one task per connection; broadcast via shared state; use DashMap for rooms
Testing_Strategy: Test message delivery; load test concurrent connections; test reconnection
Anti_Patterns: Blocking the write task, no ping/pong heartbeat, unbounded send channels, not handling disconnects

Error_Strategy: Return nb::Result for non-blocking ops; use defmt for error logging
Concurrency_Strategy: Embassy for async tasks; interrupt handlers for real-time
Testing_Strategy: Test drivers on host with embedded-hal-mock; defmt-test on device
Anti_Patterns: Using std features, heap allocation, blocking in interrupt handlers, busy-wait loops

Package_Priority: wasm-bindgen, web-sys, leptos or yew, serde, gloo
Concurrency_Strategy: JsValue> at boundaries; log with console_error_panic_hook
Testing_Strategy: Single-threaded (WASM); use component reactivity; avoid heavy compute on main thread
Decision_Rules: Test with wasm-bindgen-test in browser; test pure logic with #[test]
Severity: Including all web-sys features, large WASM binaries, not using wee_alloc, unused JS bindings

Package_Priority: tokio, sqlx, csv, serde, rayon, tracing
Error_Strategy: Collect errors per record; continue processing; report summary at end
Concurrency_Strategy: Rayon for CPU-bound transforms; tokio for I/O-bound extract/load
Testing_Strategy: Test each stage independently; use golden files for transform verification
Anti_Patterns: Loading entire files into memory, unbounded parallelism, no progress reporting, silent data loss

Package_Priority: axum, argon2, jsonwebtoken, sqlx, tokio, tracing
Error_Strategy: Never leak auth details; generic messages to client; detailed internal logging
Concurrency_Strategy: Request-scoped; rate limit login attempts; background token cleanup task
Testing_Strategy: Test token gen/verify; test password hashing with spawn_blocking; integration test auth flow
Anti_Patterns: Storing plaintext passwords, JWT without expiration, using SHA256 for passwords, leaking user existence

Package_Priority: tokio, lapin (RabbitMQ) or redis, serde, tracing
Error_Strategy: Retry with exponential backoff; dead letter after max retries; structured error logging
Concurrency_Strategy: Worker pool with JoinSet; graceful shutdown with tokio::signal
Testing_Strategy: Test handlers as pure functions; integration test with testcontainers Redis/RabbitMQ
Anti_Patterns: Unbounded retries, no idempotency, losing tasks on crash, no dead letter queue
